subject: [PATCH v2 0/4] mm: fix reclaim storms in defrag_mode

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: reviewer suggested adding a comment to explain the WRITE/READ once mechanism, which is now detached from the main barrier() function
reviews.raw_body:
Might be worth a comment to explain what the WRITE/READ once is dealing
with here since it's now detached from the main barrier(), but otherwise

Reviewed-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>

reviews.author: Brendan Jackman
reviews.summary: Reviewer Brendan Jackman noted that his ALLOC_UNMAPPED series requires similar changes to make compaction run on orders above allocation order, and agrees with the patch's approach of using the capture_control for this purpose.
reviews.raw_body:
Just a note that I'm rebasing my ALLOC_UNMAPPED series onto this, per
the discusison here I have similar needs re making compaction run on
orders above the allocation order:

https://lore.kernel.org/all/17f2f1e7-36ee-4d90-af1c-1fbca6f94025@kernel.org/

I think the way you've done it (i.e. use the capture_control for the
"other" order) is good.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that the patch series cannot be backported to stable kernel due to dependencies between patches, and suggested either making the fix a standalone cc:stable hotfix or applying cc:stable to all four patches.
reviews.raw_body:
This cc:stable fix doesn't apply to current mainline because the
preceding non-cc:stable patches get in the way.

This means that the -stable maintainers will have to ask you to redo
the fix to make it backportable.  And you'll then be preparing a patch
which hasn't had standalone testing in mainline.  All very cumbersome.

So is it practical to make the fix come first, as a cc:stable hotfix so
we can upstream the other patches in the next merge window?

(An alternative would be to put cc:stable on all four patches.  That's
a bit abusive but we do it rarely).

Either way, I'll get these into mm.git for testing while we await
review on [4/4], thanks.

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch series addresses reclaim storms in defrag_mode by allowing non-movable requests to participate in pageblock production during direct reclaim and compaction, which helps prevent pressure spikes and swap storms. The patches make adjustments to the allocator and compaction code to enable this behavior, including changes to capture control and migration source scan restrictions. The approach taken is to modify the slowpath of the allocator to invoke direct reclaim and compaction with pageblock_order when non-movable requests are involved. This has been tested in production against affected workloads for several weeks and restores OOM kill rates to baseline levels.
